ListItem.Para: Touchpad cursor control devices (CCDs) operate normally.
Tuning and control panels (TCPs) operate normally.
All switches on remaining keypad operate normally.
Repairs are made within two flight days.
Pilot with inoperative keypad must use backup EFIS/DSP feature on an upper MFD to select lower display unit formats.
Pilot with operating keypad must make all alphanumeric entries (For example: preflight data, changes to LNAV/VNAV flight plan, etc.).
In the event of enroute failure of the remaining keypad:

Ability to change the LNAV/VNAV flight plan using alphanumeric inputs will be lost. If deviation from the LNAV/VNAV flight plan is required, use the navigation display pick waypoint function, route uplink if available, or other autopilot modes.
Backup EFIS/DSP feature must be used to select lower display unit formats.

Selection of any new multifunction display format will cause the cursor to automatically move to that format. To move the cursor to an already displayed format, select a different format, then reselect the original format.

MELSubItem.Title: SYS, CDU, INFO, CHKL, COMM, ND Switches (*)

DispatchConditionRemarks.Para: May be inoperative provided all switches on opposite keypad operate normally.

OperationNote.Para: Use the EFIS/DSP backup feature in place of any inoperative switch.

MELSubItem.Title: PREV PAGE, NEXT PAGE, EXEC Switches (*)

DispatchConditionRemarks.Para: May be inoperative provided all switches on opposite keypad operate normally.

OperationNote.Para: Use the touchpad cursor control device or the rotary CURSOR CONTROL selector on the keypad for flight management page access and control.

ListItem.Para: All switches on opposite keypad operate normally.
Associated touchpad cursor control device (CCD) operates normally.

OperationNote.Para: Use the touchpad cursor control devices for all display control functions.

MELSubItem.Title: Switch Lights, Key Lights, EXEC Lights (*)

DispatchConditionRemarks.Para: One or more may be inoperative.
